data.body:
=== RESEARCH REPORT ===
=== DSO re-explore (from-scratch): brainstorm lossless + reconcile
=== agent a95e2fdb24f449f77

I now have complete coverage of all in-scope files. The catalog cites `bot-psychologist.md:53-79` for Step 5. That agent file is not in my explicit read list (the task scoped to the named files), but the catalog flags it as part of brainstorm Step 5. I'll mark that as a reconciliation note (Step 5 dispatch is confirmed in the pipeline; the bot-psychologist's internal dimensions are out of my read scope). Let me verify the one line-number-sensitive catalog claim I can't yet confirm: agent-clarity.md:24-28 (success_measurable spans). I've read that file fully. I have everything needed.

Here is my complete extraction and reconciliation.

---

# LOSSLESS Re-Extraction: All Evaluation/QC Dimensions in DSO Brainstorm Skill

Root: `~/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/`. Files read in full: `skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md`; all of `phases/` and `prompts/`; `skills/shared/docs/reviewers/{agent-clarity,scope,value}.md`; `agents/feasibility-reviewer.md`; `skills/shared/workflows/epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md`; `skills/shared/prompts/{complexity-gate,verifiable-sc-check,value-effort-scorer,scale-inference,exploration-decomposition}.md`. Line numbers are from `cat -n` of each file.

Format per item: **name** | what it evaluates | quote | file:line | where used | scoring | overlap.

---

## SECTION A — THE UNDERSTANDING GATE (Phase 1 Gate, SKILL.md ~224–318) — under-documented before; captured exhaustively

**A1. Scope-bullet validity check** | every Scope bullet must name a concrete deliverable or confirmed exclusion (no deferred-investigation language) | "A bullet is invalid if it contains any of these patterns: 'verify whether', 'check if', 'TBD', 'outcome is no changes', or 'depends on investigation'." | SKILL.md:244 | Phase 1 Gate Step 1, required *before* presenting the Understanding Summary | gate (invalid ⇒ resolve/ask/move-to-Pending-Investigation) | overlaps the catalog's "Scope-bullet validity" (1.6).

**A2. Understanding Summary structure** | the 7-field structured summary the user must confirm | "- **Problem** … - **Users** … - **Scope** … - **Access Path** … - **Surface** … - **Inputs** … - **Success** …" | SKILL.md:230–242 | Phase 1 Gate Step 1 | gate (requires user confirmation) | new framing vs catalog (catalog only names "Intent Gap Analysis :276").

**A3. Understanding Summary phrasing requirement** | exact-string closing sentence enforced | "Close the Understanding Summary with exactly this sentence: **'Does this capture your intent? If anything is off, tell me what to adjust.'** Do not paraphrase" | SKILL.md:246–248 | Phase 1 Gate Step 1 | gate (literal-string contract) | NOT in catalog — NEW.

**A4. UI Intent Detection — re-invocation guard** | skip probes if a sentinel from a prior run exists | "Check whether `$ARTIFACTS_DIR/ux-probe-fired-<epic-id>` sentinel file exists. If the sentinel file exists … skip the rest of this step" | SKILL.md:261 | Phase 1 Gate Step 1.5 | gate (idempotency) | partial overlap w/ catalog UI line (1.6).

**A5. UI keyword scan** | count surface-lexicon hits in confirmed summary → clear-ui / clear-non-ui / ambiguous | "Test the confirmed Understanding Summary text against the active surface-lexicon … Result: `clear-ui`, `clear-non-ui`, or `ambiguous`." | SKILL.md:262 (rule defined in ui-keyword-trigger.md:6–11: ≥3 hits ⇒ clear-ui, 0 ⇒ clear-non-ui, 1–2 ⇒ ambiguous) | Phase 1 Gate Step 1.5 | threshold gate | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "keyword-trigger ≥3/0/1-2").

**A6. UI classifier dispatch (ambiguous only)** | haiku classifier returns exactly `ui`/`non-ui` | "respond with exactly one word … `ui` … `non-ui`" | SKILL.md:263–267; ui-detection-classifier.md:13–22 | Phase 1 Gate Step 1.5 ambiguous branch | binary verdict (ui/non-ui); any other output ⇒ failure ⇒ fall through to non-ui | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "intent classifier ui/non-ui").

**A7. Classifier-failure degradation** | on any failure, treat as non-ui, don't set fired flag | "On any failure (agent unavailable, MAX_AGENTS=0, timeout, malformed output, or any response other than exactly `\"ui\"` or `\"non-ui\"`): … treat result as `non-ui`, and continue." | SKILL.md:267 | Phase 1 Gate Step 1.5 | fail-open default | NOT explicitly in catalog — NEW (degradation path).

**A8. Non-interactive UI deferral** | defer UX probes when non-interactive + ui | "When `BRAINSTORM_INTERACTIVE=false` and the UI detection result is `ui`: emit `INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED` … tag the epic `ui_probes:deferred` … skip all probes." | SKILL.md:269 | Phase 1 Gate Step 1.5 | gate branch | NEW.

**A9. UX Probe — criticality** | highest-stakes interactions | "Which user interactions in this feature are highest-stakes — where a mistake, error, or delay would be most disruptive to the user?" | ux-probe-set.md:9–14 | Phase 1 Gate Step 1.5 probe firing (when ui) | free-text probe (no score); suppressible by ANTI-REDUNDANCY-GATE §3 | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "criticality").

**A10. UX Probe — non_happy_path** | failure-mode coverage | "What happens when things go wrong in this flow — validation errors, timeouts, empty states, or partial data? Which failure modes are most likely to confuse users?" | ux-probe-set.md:15–20 | same | free-text probe | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "non_happy_path").

**A11. UX Probe — flow_entry_exit** | entry/exit points | "Where do users enter this flow (entry points) and where do they end up after completing or abandoning it (exit points)?" | ux-probe-set.md:21–28 | same | free-text probe | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "flow_entry_exit").

**A12. Intent Gap Analysis** | self-reflect on inferred/assumed content user didn't state; ask one targeted gap Q at a time | "After confirmation, self-reflect on inferred or assumed content — items you filled in that the user did not explicitly state." | SKILL.md:276 | Phase 1 Gate Step 2 | loop (terminates per A14) | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "Intent Gap Analysis :276").

**A13. Loop-back directive** | a gap answer revealing new understanding returns to Tell-Me-More | "When a gap answer reveals a new understanding gap … return to the Tell Me More loop (Step 2) before resuming gap analysis." | SKILL.md:290 | Phase 1 Gate Step 2 | loop control | NEW (mechanism detail).

**A14. Gap-loop termination condition** | proceed only when gap list empty AND user confirms summary | "Proceed to Phase 2 only when BOTH: (a) the gap list is empty … AND (b) the user confirms the Understanding Summary in the same turn." | SKILL.md:292 | Phase 1 Gate Step 2 | gate (two-condition) | NEW (explicit termination).

**A15. UNRESOLVABLE_INTENT_GAP escape** | when anti-redundancy suppresses all candidate gap Qs | "When the anti-redundancy directive suppresses every candidate question during loop-back … record `UNRESOLVABLE_INTENT_GAP` … and terminate Phase 1 (do NOT proceed to Phase 2)." | SKILL.md:294 | Phase 1 Gate Step 2 | escape/terminate | NEW.

**A16. Compression anti-pattern (prohibited)** | don't reframe N decisions as one "core question" | "If your draft contains 'Rather than asking', 'Instead of asking', or more than one decision sub-list under one heading, STOP — split into separate sequential questions." | SKILL.md:296 | Phase 1 Gate Step 2 (and all dialogue) | prohibition gate | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "compression anti-pattern :296").

**A17. Completeness Attestation** | compute exhausted/open field before Phase 2 | "**exhausted**: ALL gap questions are resolved … **open**: One or more gap questions remain unresolved." | SKILL.md:300–307 | Phase 1 Gate Step 2.5 | enum verdict (exhausted | open); **blocking** — gate cannot pass without it | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "Completeness Attestation exhausted/open :300-307").

**A18. Attestation blocking rule** | missing attestation = non-passing | "The Phase 1 Gate CANNOT return passed without an attestation field of value `exhausted` or `open`. A missing or absent attestation field is treated as a non-passing signal" | SKILL.md:307 | Phase 1 Gate Step 2.5 | gate | sub-detail of A17 (catalog cites the range, not this rule separately).

**A19. META_QUESTION routing** | unresolved gap whose blocking_for = brainstorm itself | "If any unresolved question's blocking_for resolves to brainstorm itself, emit META_QUESTION — NOT REPLAN_ESCALATE." | SKILL.md:309 | Phase 1 Gate Step 2.5 | routing verdict | NEW.

**A20. Anti-redundancy semantic-duplicate check** | no question whose answer was already given | "Do NOT ask any question whose answer was already given, including answers expressed through paraphrase, negative signals … or semantically-equivalent rewordings" | SKILL.md:21 | all phases, before every question | prohibition gate | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "semantic-duplicate :21-27").

**A21. Anti-redundancy codebase-discoverability check** | look in repo before asking | "Before asking a question whose answer may live in the repo, use Read, Grep, or Glob to look first." | SKILL.md:23 (also Codebase Investigation Gate :190–192, and :204) | all phases | prohibition gate | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "codebase-discoverability :190-192").

**A22. Probe suppression** | skip a UX probe topic already covered by a prior answer | "When a prior user answer covers one of the UX probe topics (criticality … non_happy_path … flow_entry_exit), skip that probe." | SKILL.md:25 | all phases | suppression gate | NEW (the catalog lists the probes but not this suppression rule).

**A23. One-question HARD-GATE** | exactly one question per user-facing message | "count the distinct questions in your draft. If the count is greater than 1, stop — select only the single highest-priority unknown and remove all others." | SKILL.md:198–200 | Phase 1 Tell-Me-More loop (and all dialogue) | hard gate | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "one-question HARD-GATE :198-200").

**A24. Multiple-choice label rule** | single ASCII letter/digit labels only | "Option labels MUST use single ASCII letters (`a`, `b`, `c`, `d`) or single digits … never Greek letters … Roman numerals … or any multi-character label." | SKILL.md:206 | Phase 1 dialogue | formatting gate (Bug 0f10-97ec) | NEW.

**A25. Exploration decomposition (SINGLE vs MULTI_SOURCE)** | classify compound context questions before answering | "classify it as SINGLE_SOURCE or MULTI_SOURCE before proceeding. Emit DECOMPOSE_RECOMMENDED when a factor is unspecified or two findings contradict." | SKILL.md:194; exploration-decomposition.md:8–69 | Codebase Investigation Gate (before user questions) | classification + DECOMPOSE_RECOMMENDED escape; FINDING confidence high/medium/low (exploration-decomposition.md:96–104) | NEW (catalog Part 1 omits this entirely).

**A26. Phase 1 Gate PRECONDITIONS write** | record decisions_log on gate exit | "write a PRECONDITIONS decisions_log entry … gate_name: phase1_gate … data: {attestation: 'exhausted', resolved_question_count: <N>}" | SKILL.md:313–318 | Phase 1 Gate exit | side-effect record (routes to 100% review bucket) | NEW.

---

## SECTION B — SCALE INFERENCE & ARCHITECTURAL CLASS (Phase 1, pre-scrutiny)

**B1. Scale inference 3-step protocol** | infer expected volume before architecture decisions | "1. Check existing artifacts … 2. Run a domain web search … 3. Ask the user only if no usable estimate is found" | SKILL.md:125–131; scale-inference.md:17–24 | Phase 1 Step 0 (on volume-sensitive trigger) | result = numeric estimate | "small scale" | "not applicable"; default = small scale (scale-inference.md:36–39) | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "Scale context (Step 0)"); detailed protocol NEW.

**B2. Upward-interpolation prohibition** | never assume higher scale than evidence supports | "Never assume a higher scale than the evidence supports. … Upward interpolation without evidence is prohibited" | scale-inference.md:42–48 | Phase 1 Step 0 | prohibition | NEW.

**B3. Epic architectural classification** | classify epic class before scrutiny | "Valid class values: `class:architectural` | `class:integration` | `class:infra` | `class:behavioral`" | SKILL.md:154–165 | after epic created / when enriching | enum classification | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "epic class architectural/integration/infra/behavioral :165").

**B4. Architectural probe gate** | architectural epics must produce Self-Use Compatibility section before scrutiny | "if ! grep -q '## Self-Use Compatibility' … echo 'PROBE_GATE_BLOCKED: probe output missing required '## Self-Use Compatibility' section'; exit 1" | SKILL.md:182–187 | Architectural Probe Dispatch (Phase 1, class:architectural only) | blocking gate (exit 1) | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "Architectural-probe gate (## Self-Use Compatibility) :182-187").

---

## SECTION C — SHAPE HEURISTIC / EXTERNAL DEPENDENCIES (Phase 1 Step 3, config-gated)

**C1. SC shape classification** | classify each SC as external-outcome via classify-sc-shape.sh | "If any SC returns `external-outcome`: … ask the user to specify `ownership`, `handling` … `claude_has_access` … `verification_command`" | SKILL.md:327–335 | Phase 1 Step 3 (when is_external_dep_block_enabled) | classification → dependency block | NEW (catalog mentions external-dep only via approval-gate contradiction).

**C2. Platform capability probe (62ae-26ec)** | probe silent GitHub/PR/CI repo-level capabilities | "When the SC mentions GitHub, PR, merge, auto-merge, branch protection, CI/required-checks, or release tagging, additionally probe these repo-level capabilities — they are silent dependencies" (auto-merge, branch protection, required status checks, merge methods) | SKILL.md:337–343 | Phase 1 Step 3 | dependency-entry generation (ownership/handling/claude_has_access) | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "Platform-capability probes … :337-343").

**C3. Release-infrastructure compatibility check (3dc2-ad99)** | flag scripts/release.sh when new package/CI/entry-point added | "if the epic introduces a new Python package … or changes that will eventually be exercised at release time … flag `scripts/release.sh` as a release-infrastructure dependency." | SKILL.md:345 | Phase 1 Step 3 (regardless of SC shape) | flag/note | NEW.

**C4. External Dependencies Contradiction Gate** | block approval when handling=claude_auto but claude_has_access=no/unknown | "Scan each entry for contradictions: an entry where `handling: claude_auto` AND `claude_has_access` is `no` or `unknown`. If any contradiction is found: Do NOT present approval gate options." | approval-gate.md:28–47 | Phase 2 Step 4 approval gate (when external_dependency_block_enabled) | blocking gate | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "contradiction gate approval-gate.md:28-47").

---

## SECTION D — PHASE 1.5 UI-COPY DETECTOR

**D1. UI-Copy signal scan (9 signals)** | detect user-visible copy needing UX copywriting review | 9-row table: form fields/validation; error messages/states; button labels/CTA; instructional/helper text; microcopy; accessibility copy; plain-language/federal style; confirmation/status strings; user-visible strings in AC | SKILL.md:364–377 | Phase 1.5 Step 1 | result = `copy-detected` / `copy-not-detected` (single match suffices) | CONFIRMED catalog (1.6 "UI-copy 9-signal scan SKILL.md:364-377").

**D2. Copy-needs idempotency guard** | skip if already tagged + section present | "Both tag and section present — Phase 1.5 is a no-op" | SKILL.md:379–399 | Phase 1.5 Step 2 | gate (no-op / write-section / proceed) | NEW (sub-detail).

---

## SECTION E — PHASE 2 SC DRAFTING RULES + verifiable-sc-check

**E1. SC count 3–6** | "3–6 criteria per epic" | SKILL.md:550 | Phase 2 Step 2 | threshold | CONFIRMED catalog (1.5 ":549" — note: catalog says :549, actual is :550; off-by-one).
**E2. SC verifiable pass/fail** | "Each must be verifiable pass/fail" | SKILL.md:551 | Phase 2 Step 2 | rule | CONFIRMED catalog (":550", off-by-one).
**E3. SC session-verifiable** | "the pass/fail verdict must be renderable before the sprint closes" | SKILL.md:552 | Phase 2 Step 2 | rule | CONFIRMED catalog (":551", off-by-one).
**E4. verifiable-SC check applied per SC** | apply session-infeasibility litmus | "Apply the **verifiable-SC check** … session-infeasible SCs are prohibited" | SKILL.md:553 | Phase 2 Step 2 | gate; remediation = rewrite proxy / DEFERRED_MEASUREMENT / Closure Checks | CONFIRMED catalog (1.5 "Session-feasibility self-check verifiable-sc-check.md:11-30").
**E5. SC outcomes-not-implementation** | "Describe outcomes, not implementation" | SKILL.md:554 | Phase 2 Step 2 | rule | CONFIRMED catalog (":553", off-by-one).
**E6. ≥1 validation-signal hint** | "At least one criterion should hint at a validation signal" | SKILL.md:555 | Phase 2 Step 2 | rule | CONFIRMED catalog (":554", off-by-one). Overlaps value.md validation_signal.
**E7. Executable-artifact end-to-end rule** | CI/Actions/etc. need ≥1 SC exercising the actual artifact end-to-end | "include at least one SC that exercises the artifact end-to-end in one of: (a) integration test … (b) … shadow landing … (c) live invocation" | SKILL.md:555 (multi-line; catalog says :555) | Phase 2 Step 2 | rule | CONFIRMED catalog (":555").
**E8. Superseding-is-never-an-SC** | ticket bookkeeping is not an SC | "**Superseding or closing another epic is NEVER an SC.**" | SKILL.md:556 | Phase 2 Step 2 | prohibition | CONFIRMED catalog (":556").
**E9. Context-narrative rules** | name user / today's problem / no jargon | SKILL.md:560–564 | Phase 2 Step 2 | rules | CONFIRMED catalog (1.5 ":561/:562/:564"). Overlaps value.md user_impact.

**E10. Session-infeasibility self-check** | can SC be evaluated in the closing session? | "Can this criterion be evaluated during the session that closes the epic, using only (a) code/artifacts … (b) CI/test results … or (c) a command …" | verifiable-sc-check.md:11–30 | Phase 2 Step 2 per SC | YES/NO gate | CONFIRMED catalog (1.5).
**E11. End-state-only litmus** | route transitions to Closure Checks | "Could this item be false before the sprint began and true only because of this sprint's specific work? If yes, route to Closure Checks." | verifiable-sc-check.md:50–66 | Phase 2 Step 2 | route decision (SC vs Closure Check) | CONFIRMED catalog (1.5).
**E12. Verify-Intent 3-element requirement** | each SC's Verify-intent has subject+action+observable | "Every Verify-intent must contain three elements: 1. Subject … 2. Action … 3. Observable" | verifiable-sc-check.md:78–86 | Phase 2 Step 2.15 (batch after SCs) | reject if any element missing | CONFIRMED catalog (1.5 "Verify-Intent 3-element verifiable-sc-check.md:78-86").
**E13. Closure-Check one-shot-verifiable requirement** | each Closure Check phrased as command / state-check / named-artifact ref; reject human-judgment items | "REJECT items that describe human activity, judgment, or aggregate feedback" | epic-description-template.md:21–31 (catalog cited :21-31) | Phase 2/3 spec | gate | CONFIRMED catalog (1.5 "Closure-Check one-shot-verifiable epic-description-template.md:21-31").

**E14. State-Lifecycle Owner block** | CREATE/UPDATE/CONSUME/RETIRE owners for shared state; unowned RETIRE = defect | "RETIRE must always be filled in … an unowned RETIRE row is itself a state-lifecycle defect" | SKILL.md:579–589 | Phase 2 Step 2 (conditional, bug 3f3d) | rule/gate | CONFIRMED catalog (1.5 ":579-589").

**E15. Provenance classification** | classify each SC origin: explicit / confirmed-via-gap-question / inferred / researched / injected | "**explicit** … **confirmed-via-gap-question** … **inferred** … **researched** … **injected**" | SKILL.md:565–577 | Phase 2 (tracked internally, used at approval gate) | 5-category classification (drives bold annotation) | NEW (catalog doesn't list provenance classes).

---

## SECTION F — COMPLEXITY GATE (Phase 2 Step 0; complexity-gate.md) — Gates 1–6 explicitly

**F1. Gate 1 YAGNI** | block complexity for absent future needs | "FAIL if the proposed feature/abstraction addresses a requirement absent from the current story." | complexity-gate.md:26–38 | Phase 2 proposals | PASS/FAIL | CONFIRMED catalog (3.9/1.5 "Gate1 YAGNI :26").
**F2. Gate 2 Rule of Three** | no abstraction <3 existing call sites | "FAIL if fewer than 3 existing call sites." | complexity-gate.md:40–48 | Phase 2 proposals (abstraction <3 call sites) | PASS/FAIL | CONFIRMED catalog ("Gate2 Rule-of-Three :40").
**F3. Gate 3 Dependency Cost/Benefit** | new lib must be >30 LOC inline OR correctness/security guarantee | "FAIL if neither condition (a) nor condition (b) is met." | complexity-gate.md:50–60 | Phase 2 proposals (new library) | PASS/FAIL | CONFIRMED catalog ("Gate3 Dependency :50").
**F4. Gate 4 Scale Threshold** | perf optimization needs scale estimate/profiling | "FAIL if no scale evidence or profiling result is cited." | complexity-gate.md:62–73 | Phase 2 (perf proposals); scale context propagated SKILL.md:505 | PASS/FAIL | CONFIRMED catalog ("Gate4 Scale Threshold :61"; actual :62).
**F5. Gate 5 Profiling-First** | no perf optimization without measured baseline | "FAIL if the optimization is proposed without citing a benchmark." | complexity-gate.md:74–84 | Phase 2 perf proposals | PASS/FAIL (complementary to Gate 4) | CONFIRMED catalog ("Gate5 Profiling-First :74").
**F6. Gate 6 LLM Self-Audit** | structured 3-question over-engineering reflection | "The self-audit does not produce PASS/FAIL — it produces a reflection that must be included in the proposal rationale." | complexity-gate.md:86–96 | Phase 2 proposals | reflection (no verdict) | CONFIRMED catalog ("Gate6 LLM Self-Audit :86").
**F7. Sandbagging Prohibition** | simple baseline must be genuinely viable | "Describing a technically inadequate option while loading the description with scalability caveats unless those caveats are grounded in Phase 1 scale evidence … is prohibited." | complexity-gate.md:108–114 | Phase 2 Step 1 (simple baseline requirement, SKILL.md:501) | prohibition | CONFIRMED catalog ("Sandbagging Prohibition :108").
**F8. Complexity gate for proposals (GATE/CHECKED/FINDING/VERDICT)** | any proposal w/ new dep, perf opt, or <3-call-site abstraction must include the block | "Any proposal that includes (a) a new library dependency, (b) a performance optimization, or (c) an abstraction with fewer than 3 existing call sites must include a GATE/CHECKED/FINDING/VERDICT block" | SKILL.md:503 | Phase 2 Step 1 | structured PASS/FAIL block; FAIL ⇒ remove/revise unless justified-complexity path | partial overlap (catalog lists gates but not this dispatch trigger).
**F9. Justified-Complexity Path** | override a FAILed gate with affirmative evidence | "it requires affirmative evidence, not an absence of disqualifying evidence." | complexity-gate.md:98–106 | Phase 2 when a gate FAILs but complexity warranted | escape (evidence-gated) | NEW.

---

## SECTION G — CROSS-EPIC INTERACTION SCAN (Step 2.25 + handlers)

**G1. Cross-epic interaction classifier (haiku)** | detect shared-resource conflicts vs other open/in-progress epics | "dispatches haiku-tier classifier agents and collects `CROSS_EPIC_SIGNALS`" | SKILL.md:602–621; cross-epic-scan.md:1–130 | Phase 2 Step 2.25 | severity tiers: benign / consideration / ambiguity / conflict | NEW (catalog Part 1 omits cross-epic scan entirely).
**G2. Severity routing** | route signals by tier | "benign: log … consideration: … AC injection … ambiguity or conflict: … halt/resolution" | SKILL.md:616–619; cross-epic-scan.md:110–122 | Step 2.25g | routing | NEW.
**G3. Consideration AC injection** | construct structured AC (shared resource + epic + falsifiable constraint) | "Construct a structured AC with these three required fields" | cross-epic-handlers.md:12–27 | Step 2.26 | injected-provenance AC | NEW.
**G4. Workflow-trigger filter audit (Step 2.26b)** | confirm `.github/workflows/*.yml` triggers cover a new git ref pattern | "When a workflow's trigger filter would silently skip the new ref pattern … add a finding" | SKILL.md:621; cross-epic-handlers.md:31–78 | Step 2.26b (when epic introduces new git ref pattern) | INCLUDED/EXCLUDED/NO_FILTER checklist + gap flag | NEW.
**G5. Ambiguity/conflict halt & resolution (Step 2.27)** | halt and present resolve/override/halt options | "(a) Resolve … (b) Override … (c) Halt" | cross-epic-handlers.md:82–118 | Step 2.27 | user-choice gate; tags `interaction:deferred` | NEW.
**G6. Relates-to AC injection (Step 2.28)** | inject AC w/ URL-navigability classification + Playwright assertion | "**Navigable URL** … **Non-URL resource**" → 4-field vs 3-field AC | cross-epic-handlers.md:122–157 | Step 2.28 (after SC Gap Check) | classification + AC injection | NEW.

---

## SECTION H — EPIC SCRUTINY PIPELINE (Steps 2.5–3) — gap analysis Parts A/B/C

**H1. Part A — Artifact Contradiction Detection** | user-named artifacts missing from SCs (fuzzy match) | "Identify any artifacts, files, components, or named concepts that the user explicitly named … but that are absent from or not covered by the success criteria." | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:30–53 | Step 2.5 Part A | gate (present gaps; user resolves) | CONFIRMED catalog (1.2 ":30-53").
**H2. Part B — sync loops** | bidirectional flow → dup/false-conflict/loop | "If the feature involves bidirectional data flow … will it create duplicates, false conflicts, or infinite loops?" | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:59 | Step 2.5 Part B | finding | CONFIRMED catalog (1.2 ":59").
**H3. Part B — race conditions** | "If multiple actors … can modify the same state concurrently, what happens when they collide?" | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:60 | Part B | finding | CONFIRMED (":60").
**H4. Part B — invalidated assumptions** | "Will adding new data to an existing format break hashing, parsing, caching, or diffing" | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:61 | Part B | finding | CONFIRMED (":61").
**H5. Part B — parsing ambiguities** | "can user-provided content contain those same markers?" | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:62 | Part B | finding | CONFIRMED (":62").
**H6. Part B — deprecation/rename reference sweep** | grep old paths + report count | "Run a codebase grep for references to any deprecated path and report the count." | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:63 | Part B | finding+count | CONFIRMED (":63").
**H7. Part B — Inference-Signal Scan** | hedging/confident trigger-words ⇒ MUST Read/Grep-verify inferred input source | "scan the **Inputs bullet** … for trigger words that indicate an inferred input source" + "**MUST-execute** `Read` or `Grep` verification" | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:65–84 | Part B | verified/gap; cached context NOT a substitute (:82); contract-doc ground-truth rule (:80) | CONFIRMED catalog (1.2 ":65-84").
**H8. Part C — Shared Artifact Impact (file-artifact mode)** | file consumed by 2+ external files OR moved/deprecated/removed/renamed | "when the Success Criteria section … references creating or modifying a file that is consumed by '2+ other files' outside its own directory, or references moving, deprecating, removing, or renaming" | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:88–115 | Step 2.5 Part C | emits `covered_by_SC` boolean tuples; feeds consumer_completeness | CONFIRMED catalog (1.2 ":88-115").
**H9. Part C — behavioral-prohibition mode** | new gate/restriction → grep existing call sites of newly-forbidden behavior | "when an SC introduces a new enforcement, restriction, or gate that prohibits a previously-permitted action" | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:93,100–106 | Part C | covered_by_SC tuples | CONFIRMED catalog (mentions "behavioral-prohibition mode" in 1.2).
**H10. Part C extension — cross-epic relates_to suggestion** | discovered artifacts overlapping other open epics → suggest relates_to link | "check whether any of the artifact files discovered overlap with other open epics" | epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:117–157 | Part C extension | user-approval gate before link creation | CONFIRMED catalog (1.2 ":117-157").

**H11. ## SECTION I — FIDELITY REVIEWS (Step 4) — all scored dimensions w/ both rubric columns

Pass threshold: **all dimensions ≥4** (epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:311). Scale 1–5 + N/A (each reviewer's Scoring Scale table). Re-run failing reviewer until pass or escalate (:327–330). Anti-patterns guarded: "current vs. future state" anchor (:332), conflict-detection escalation (:334).

**Agent Clarity reviewer** (agent-clarity.md; perspective `"Agent Clarity"`):
**I1. self_contained** | "A planner can decompose the spec into stories without asking clarifying questions" (4-5) vs "relies on undefined jargon, acronyms, or references to undocumented systems" (<4) | agent-clarity.md:23 | 1-5/N/A | CONFIRMED catalog (1.1).
**I2. success_measurable** | "Every success criterion describes a specific, observable outcome verifiable at the feature level" (4-5) vs "subjective language … describe effort rather than outcomes … vague outcomes" (<4); post-sprint measurement ⇒ score ≤2 (:28); observability tooling is valid (:26) | agent-clarity.md:24–28 | 1-5/N/A | CONFIRMED catalog (1.1 ":24-28"). Overlaps E2/E3/E5.

**Scope reviewer** (scope.md; perspective `"Scope"`):
**I3. right_sized** | coherent single-sprint unit (4-5) vs epic-of-epics or trivial task (<4); too-large signals (2+ user goals, exceeds quarter), too-small signals (one-sprint-one-dev, single SC) | scope.md:23 | 1-5/N/A | CONFIRMED (1.1).
**I4. no_overlap** | "scope is clearly differentiated from all other milestones" (4-5) vs "duplicate deliverables already claimed by another milestone" (<4) | scope.md:24 | 1-5/N/A | CONFIRMED. Overlaps G1 (cross-epic).
**I5. dependency_aware** | "milestones this one depends on … named explicitly … including why" (4-5) vs "implicitly require capabilities … dependency not called out" (<4) | scope.md:25 | 1-5/N/A | CONFIRMED.
**I6. consumer_completeness** | "All consumers of artifacts … (discovered by Part C scan) are covered by the success criteria" (4-5) vs "Part C scan reveals consumers not covered by any success criterion and no descoping rationale"; N/A when Part C skipped | scope.md:26 | 1-5/N/A | CONFIRMED. Overlaps H8/H9 (Part C).

**Value reviewer** (value.md; perspective `"Value"`):
**I7. user_impact** | "Context narrative names a specific user … describes the problem they face today … observable improvement" (4-5) vs "technical task with no named user … system internals with no user-visible impact" (<4) | value.md:24 | 1-5/N/A | CONFIRMED. Overlaps E9.
**I8. validation_signal** | "At least one success criterion includes a concrete mechanism for validating … executable within the sprint session" (4-5) vs "All success criteria describe system outputs … with no plan to verify"; N/A for purely internal work (<4) | value.md:25 | 1-5/N/A | CONFIRMED. Overlaps E6.

**Feasibility reviewer** (feasibility-reviewer.md; perspective `"Technical Feasibility"`; conditional 4th — triggered by integration signals / first-time internal platform feature, pipeline:294–309):
**I9. technical_feasibility** | confidence integrations are achievable | "5 = All integration signals verified — official docs + working GitHub examples … 1 = One or more signals contradicted" | feasibility-reviewer.md:134–144 | 1-5/N/A | CONFIRMED (1.1).
**I10. integration_risk** | risk integration failures block/delay (higher=lower risk) | "5 = No integration risk … 1 = Critical risk — one or more integrations are unverified or contradicted; spike required" | feasibility-reviewer.md:146–157 | 1-5/N/A; any feasibility score <3 ⇒ `## FEASIBILITY_GAP` + return to caller (pipeline:313) | CONFIRMED (1.1).

Feasibility sub-checks (CONFIRMED catalog 1.1 sub-line):
**I11. Capability-gap classification** | verified / partially verified / unverified / contradicted | feasibility-reviewer.md:104–112 | enum | CONFIRMED.
**I12. CLI subcommand surface (Step 1b)** | per-command `--help` empirical verification | "Flag any mismatch … as a **critical capability gap**" → verdict MATCH/MISMATCH/UNVERIFIED | feasibility-reviewer.md:52–67, 175–188 | MATCH/MISMATCH/UNVERIFIED per command; empty command_surface ⇒ treated as unverified | CONFIRMED.
**I13. Auth HTTPS precondition** | OAuth/OIDC require HTTPS; HTTP-only ⇒ Contradicted | feasibility-reviewer.md:114–116 | gate (critical capability gap) | CONFIRMED.
